# ChIP-Atlas

> ChIP-Atlas is a comprehensive database of public epigenomic data (ChIP-seq, ATAC-seq, DNase-seq, Bisulfite-seq, CUT&Tag, CUT&RUN) covering over 1M experiments across seven genome assemblies. It provides peak browsing, colocalization analysis, target gene prediction, enrichment analysis, and differential analysis.

ChIP-Atlas integrates data from NCBI SRA, GEO, and other public repositories. All experiments are uniformly processed and classified by genome, track class, track subclass, cell type class, and cell type subclass.

## Main Features

- [Peak Browser](https://chip-atlas.org/peak_browser): Browse and download assembled peak-call data filtered by genome, track class, target, cell type, and significance threshold
- [Enrichment Analysis](https://chip-atlas.org/enrichment_analysis): Test whether your genomic regions are enriched for specific ChIP-seq/ATAC-seq signals
- [Differential Analysis](https://chip-atlas.org/diff_analysis): Compare epigenomic profiles between sample groups (DiffBind / DMR)
- [Target Genes](https://chip-atlas.org/target_genes): Predict target genes regulated by specific transcription factors or histone marks
- [Colocalization](https://chip-atlas.org/colo): Analyze genome-wide colocalization between epigenomic factors
- [Experiment Search](https://chip-atlas.org/search): Search all experiments by keyword, accession ID, or classification

## API for Agents

- [Agent Guide](https://chip-atlas.org/agents): Full documentation of MCP tools, HTTP API endpoints, data model, and usage workflows for AI agents
- [OpenAPI 3.1 Spec](https://chip-atlas.org/openapi.yaml): Machine-readable API specification for all JSON endpoints
- [MCP Server Source](https://github.com/inutano/chip-atlas/tree/master/mcp): TypeScript MCP server providing read-only tools for querying ChIP-Atlas programmatically

## API Endpoints

All endpoints are under the `/api/` prefix, return JSON, and use snake_case field names.

### Classification (cacheable)

- [List Genomes](https://chip-atlas.org/api/genomes): GET — available genome assemblies: hg38, mm10, rn6, dm6, ce11, sacCer3, TAIR10
- [List Track Classes](https://chip-atlas.org/api/track_classes): GET — static list of track types; with `?genome=hg38&cell_type_class=Blood` returns types with experiment counts
- [List Cell Type Classes](https://chip-atlas.org/api/cell_type_classes?genome=hg38&track_class=Histone): GET — cell type classes with counts for a genome and track class
- [List Track Subclasses](https://chip-atlas.org/api/track_subclasses?genome=hg38&track_class=Histone&cell_type_class=Blood): GET — specific targets (H3K4me3, CTCF, etc.) with counts
- [List Cell Type Subclasses](https://chip-atlas.org/api/cell_type_subclasses?genome=hg38&track_class=Histone&cell_type_class=Blood): GET — specific cell types/lines (K-562, HeLa, etc.) with counts

### Data

- [Experiment Metadata](https://chip-atlas.org/api/experiment?experiment_id=SRX018625): GET — full metadata for one experiment by accession ID
- [Search Experiments](https://chip-atlas.org/api/search?q=H3K4me3&genome=hg38&limit=20&offset=0): GET — full-text search with pagination (replaces bulk JSON dumps)
- [Genome Index](https://chip-atlas.org/api/genome_index): GET — full facet index for all genomes (large, cached 1hr)
- [Q-value Range](https://chip-atlas.org/api/qval_range): GET — available q-value thresholds (e.g. "05", "10", "20", "50")
- [BED Sizes](https://chip-atlas.org/api/bed_sizes): GET — peak count statistics
- [Colocalization Index](https://chip-atlas.org/api/colo_index?genome=hg38): GET — available colocalization analyses for a genome
- [Target Genes Index](https://chip-atlas.org/api/target_genes_index): GET — available target gene analyses by genome

### URL Generation (GET for agents, POST for frontend)

- [Download URL](https://chip-atlas.org/api/download_url?genome=hg38&track_class=Histone&track_subclass=H3K4me3&cell_type_class=Blood&cell_type_subclass=-&qval=05): GET — BED file download URL
- [IGV URL](https://chip-atlas.org/api/igv_url?genome=hg38&track_class=Histone&track_subclass=H3K4me3&cell_type_class=Blood&cell_type_subclass=-&qval=05): GET — IGV genome browser URL
- [Colocalization URLs](https://chip-atlas.org/api/colo_urls?genome=hg38&track=CTCF&cell_type=K-562): GET — colocalization result URLs (data_url, tsv_url, gml_url)
- [Target Genes URLs](https://chip-atlas.org/api/target_genes_urls?genome=hg38&track=CTCF&distance=5000): GET — target genes result URLs (data_url, tsv_url)

### Jobs

- [Service Status](https://chip-atlas.org/status): GET — check data server, WABI, WES backend availability and feature status
- [Job Availability](https://chip-atlas.org/jobs/available?type=enrichment_analysis): GET — check if a compute backend is up for a job type
- [Submit Job](https://chip-atlas.org/jobs/submit): POST — submit an enrichment or differential analysis job
- [Job Status](https://chip-atlas.org/jobs/:id/status?backend=wabi): GET — poll job status
- [Job Result](https://chip-atlas.org/jobs/:id/result?backend=wabi): GET — get result URLs for a completed job
- [Job Log](https://chip-atlas.org/jobs/:id/log?backend=wabi): GET — get execution log

## Data Model

Experiments are classified along these dimensions: genome assembly (hg38, mm10, rn6, dm6, ce11, sacCer3, TAIR10), track class (Histone, TFs and others, RNA polymerase, Input control, ATAC-Seq, DNase-seq, Bisulfite-Seq, CUT&Tag, CUT&RUN), track subclass (H3K4me3, CTCF, etc.), cell type class (Blood, Brain, Liver, etc.), and cell type subclass (K-562, HeLa, etc.). Each experiment has an SRX, ERX, or DRX accession ID.

All field names use snake_case: experiment_id, genome, track_class, track_subclass, cell_type_class, cell_type_subclass, cell_type_subclass_info, read_info, title, attributes.

Classification endpoints return arrays of `{id, label, count}` objects. Search returns `{total, returned, experiments: [...]}`.

## Important Notes for Agents

### Data architecture

ChIP-Atlas consists of two components: the **app server** (chip-atlas.org) serving the web UI and APIs, and the **data backend** (chip-atlas.dbcls.jp) hosting pre-computed result files. The app server generates URLs pointing to the data backend. Download URLs, colocalization results, and target gene results are all served from chip-atlas.dbcls.jp, not the app server itself.

### Service status

Before submitting jobs or generating URLs, check `GET /status` to verify the data server and compute backends are reachable. The response includes `data_server`, `wabi`, `wes` booleans and a `feature_status` map. If a backend is down, the corresponding analysis features will be unavailable.

### Peak Browser download

`GET /api/download_url` returns a URL to a pre-computed BED file on the data backend. The `qval` parameter must match a pre-computed threshold. Use `GET /api/qval_range` to retrieve valid values (typically "05", "10", "20", "50"). Other qval values will return `{"url": null}`.

The download URL requires all of: `genome`, `track_class`, `track_subclass`, `cell_type_class`, `cell_type_subclass` (use "-" for unspecified), and `qval`.

### Colocalization and Target Genes results

`GET /api/colo_urls` and `GET /api/target_genes_urls` return URLs to pre-computed result files on the data backend. Not all track/cell type combinations have pre-computed results. A 404 from the result URL means that combination has no data available, not that the API is broken. Use the colocalization and target genes index endpoints to check which combinations have data before requesting results.

### Experiment Search

`GET /api/search` performs full-text search across the experiment database with pagination (`limit` and `offset` parameters). This replaces the previous bulk JSON experiment list. The search index is populated at app startup from the experiment metadata. If the index is empty, the endpoint returns `{"total": 0, "returned": 0, "experiments": []}`. This does not indicate an error.

### Jobs API

The jobs API supports enrichment analysis and differential analysis. The workflow is:
1. Check availability: `GET /jobs/available?type=enrichment_analysis`
2. Submit: `POST /jobs/submit` with job type and parameters
3. Poll status: `GET /jobs/:id/status?backend=wabi`
4. Retrieve results: `GET /jobs/:id/result?backend=wabi`
5. Optionally check logs: `GET /jobs/:id/log?backend=wabi`

## Optional

- [Publications](https://chip-atlas.org/publications): Papers citing ChIP-Atlas
- [Documentation Wiki](https://github.com/inutano/chip-atlas/wiki): Detailed usage guide
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/inutano/chip-atlas): Source code
